name: South Korea
summary: Critical endpoint of helium-semiconductor cascade. KOSPI -10.6% monthly ‚Äî TRIPLE CASCADE: helium‚Üísemiconductor fabs (~40%), Google TurboQuant‚Üímemory demand (~25%), foreign capital flight (~35%). Foreigners dumped 51.4 trillion won ($37.8B) since February ‚Äî largest since GFC. Samsung foreign ownership at 48.9% ‚Äî lowest since October 2013 (12.5-year low). Won at 1,508/USD ‚Äî weakest since March 2009 (17-year low). Helium inventory exhaustion ~8 days away (early April).

signals.content: KOSPI crashed 6.5% triggering circuit breaker ‚Äî worst single-day drop since COVID. Samsung Electronics fell 8.2%. SK Hynix fell 9.1%. Driven by oil spike + helium supply concerns + global risk-off. (Sources: yfinance, Bloomberg, Reuters)

KOSPI at 5,438.87 (-0.4% daily, -10.6% monthly). Two semiconductor fabs announced reduced output due to helium allocation restrictions following Ras Laffan damage. USD/KRW at 1,508 ‚Äî weakest since 2022. South Korea's energy import dependence (~70% from Gulf) makes it acutely exposed to Hormuz disruption. (Sources: yfinance, industry reports)

KOSPI triple cascade confirmed: helium‚Üísemiconductor fabs (~40% of -10.6% monthly decline, ~4.2%), Google TurboQuant AI memory compression (~25%, ~2.7%), foreign capital flight (~35%, ~3.7%). Foreigners dumped 51.4 trillion won ($37.8B) since February ‚Äî largest outflow since Global Financial Crisis. Samsung foreign ownership hit 48.9% ‚Äî lowest since October 2013 (12.5-year low). Won at 1,508/USD ‚Äî weakest since March 2009 (17-year low). TrendForce's March 24 'two-week clock' on helium inventories means ~8 days remain before Asian chip fab inventories exhaust at full production rates. The helium cascade path (war‚ÜíQatar‚Üíhelium‚Üísemiconductors‚ÜíKOSPI) validated by 3 independent sources (TrendForce, Nikkei Asia, Korea International Trade Association). (Sources: yfinance, TrendForce, Korea International Trade Association)

edges.context: Samsung and SK Hynix depend on helium for semiconductor fabrication. Qatar supplies ~25% of global helium. Ras Laffan damage directly disrupts Korean chip production.
South Korea imports ~70% of energy from the Gulf. Oil above $110 adds massive import costs. Direct exposure to Hormuz disruption.
Majority of Korean energy imports transit Hormuz. Closure directly impacts energy security and industrial production.

trigger_points.condition: Samsung or SK Hynix announces production halt due to helium shortage
trigger_points.mechanism: Confirmed fab shutdown would cascade to global semiconductor supply, AI infrastructure, and tech valuations worldwide. Currently only 'reduced output' reported, not full halt.
